frameworks.name: linear ballistic accumulator
frameworks.one_line: evidence-accumulation decision model fitted to behavioral training data

frameworks.name: perceptual learning plasticity
frameworks.one_line: training effects extended from early visual cortex to decision regions

frameworks.name: early visual cortex plasticity
frameworks.one_line: classical view localizing learning to early sensory areas

frameworks.name: model-based fMRI
frameworks.one_line: links model parameters to BOLD responses across networks

core_question: How does perceptual learning modulate decision-related brain regions in the human brain?
key_assumptions: behavioral improvement decomposes into model parameters such as drift rate
drift rate indexes sensory information accumulation
spatial independent components isolate functional networks
manipulations: training on a motion direction discrimination task
key_findings: behavioral improvement explained by improved sensory information accumulation (drift rate)
drift rate correlated with fMRI signals in PMv, SEF, and fronto-parietal network
direction-specific signal enhancement in PMv and FEF, reduction in V3A and MT+
strengthened effective connectivity from V3A to PMv and from IPS to FEF
interpretations: perceptual learning induces decision refinement in decision-related regions
training reshapes sensory-to-decision routing across cortical hierarchy
